title: Extracurricular decision protocol with fairness concerns
scenario_summary: Two divorced parents keep clashing over how extracurricular commitments are decided and communicated. The main conflict is not schedule logistics, but fairness, notice, and not being sidelined in child-related decisions.
intended_challenge_type: Communication-process design plus fairness-sensitive option packaging without unnecessary escalation.

public_goals: Have enough notice before extracurricular commitments are finalized.
Avoid feeling blindsided by activity decisions.
Use a predictable co-parent communication process.
starting_positions: Primary position: extracurricular commitments should not be finalized without enough notice to discuss them first.
Secondary position: written summaries should follow important child-activity decisions.

public_goals: Avoid being sidelined in child-activity decisions.
Keep the process fair and workable rather than rigid.
Reduce repeated conflict over how decisions are communicated.
starting_positions: Primary position: child-activity decisions should not leave one parent feeling sidelined or informed after the fact.
Secondary position: written summaries are acceptable if they support fairness rather than control.
